---
title: "Klasifikasi Perokok Berat di Provinsi Jambi Menggunakan Machine Learning (Model v3 - R1209 & Fitur Baru)"
author: "Tim Data Science"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight-style: tango
    code-fold: show
    code-tools: true
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

# 1. Pendahuluan

Dokumen ini mendemonstrasikan **Model v3** dari klasifikasi Kepala Rumah Tangga (KRT) Perokok Berat di Provinsi Jambi. Pada versi ini, kita memperkenalkan beberapa perbaikan utama:
1. **Raw DBF Loading**: Data dibaca langsung dari file mentah `.dbf` Susenas Nasional (`ssn202403_kor_ind1.dbf` dan `ssn202403_kor_rt.dbf`) untuk menghindari data post-processed yang hilang.
2. **Fitur Baru Hasil Riset Korelasi**: Memasukkan `R1209` ("Apakah dulu pernah merokok tembakau") yang memiliki korelasi Spearman tertinggi (-0.467) terhadap target, serta beberapa fitur ekonomi, ijazah, dan jam kerja baru.
3. **Gender-Split Modeling**: Karena KRT Perempuan memiliki tingkat prevalensi perokok berat yang sangat rendah (~1%), model dilatih khusus pada sub-populasi KRT Laki-laki. Sub-populasi Perempuan langsung diprediksi sebagai `0` (Bukan Perokok Berat).
4. **Optimasi Threshold Berbasis OOF**: Threshold klasifikasi ditentukan dengan memaksimalan Balanced Accuracy secara keseluruhan menggunakan prediksi Out-of-Fold (OOF) pada data training.

---

# 2. Persiapan & Preprocessing Data

```{r setup_library}
library(foreign)     # Membaca file DBF
library(dplyr)       # Manipulasi data
library(tidyr)       # Perapihan data
library(ggplot2)     # Visualisasi
library(caret)       # Machine Learning & Evaluasi
library(ranger)      # Random Forest cepat
library(xgboost)     # eXtreme Gradient Boosting
library(pROC)        # Analisis Kurva ROC
```

## 2.1 Load Data Mentah DBF Jambi

Kita membaca file DBF nasional langsung di dalam R dan memfilternya untuk Provinsi Jambi (`R101 == "15"`).

```{r load_raw_data}
cat("Membaca file DBF Individu...\n")
ind_dbf <- read.dbf("data/ssn202403_kor_ind1.dbf", as.is = TRUE)
jambi_ind <- ind_dbf %>% filter(R101 == "15")

cat("Membaca file DBF Rumah Tangga...\n")
rt_dbf <- read.dbf("data/ssn202403_kor_rt.dbf", as.is = TRUE)
jambi_rt <- rt_dbf %>% filter(R101 == "15")

cat("Dimensi awal Individu Jambi:", dim(jambi_ind)[1], "baris,", dim(jambi_ind)[2], "kolom.\n")
cat("Dimensi awal Rumah Tangga Jambi:", dim(jambi_rt)[1], "baris,", dim(jambi_rt)[2], "kolom.\n")
```

## 2.2 Penyaringan KRT & Pembentukan Target (Y)

Target `Y = 1` didefinisikan sebagai KRT (`R403 == "1"`) yang merokok sebulan terakhir (`R1207 == "1"`) dengan konsumsi $\ge 140$ batang seminggu (`R1208` $\ge 140$).

```{r process_target}
jambi_krt <- jambi_ind %>%
  filter(R403 == "1") %>%
  mutate(
    r1208_num = suppressWarnings(as.integer(R1208)),
    Y = case_when(
      R1207 %in% c("5", "2") ~ 0,
      R1207 == "1" & !is.na(r1208_num) & r1208_num >= 140 ~ 1,
      R1207 == "1" & !is.na(r1208_num) & r1208_num < 140 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Y))

cat("Jumlah KRT dengan status target valid:", nrow(jambi_krt), "\n")
```

## 2.3 Penggabungan Data (Merging)

```{r merge_data}
join_keys <- c("R101", "R102", "R105", "WI1", "WI2", "PSU", "SSU", "URUT")
df_merged <- left_join(jambi_krt, jambi_rt, by = join_keys)
```

## 2.4 Seleksi & Rekayasa Fitur

Kita menyaring fitur yang relevan, terutama variabel baru hasil analisis korelasi (seperti `R1209` untuk riwayat merokok dan `R614` untuk ijazah tertinggi).

```{r select_features}
to_num <- function(x) {
  suppressWarnings(as.numeric(ifelse(x %in% c("", "."), NA_character_, x)))
}

df_features <- df_merged %>%
  mutate(
    # Continuous features
    umur_krt = to_num(R407),
    jumlah_art = to_num(R1801),
    luas_lantai = to_num(R1804),
    jam_kerja_krt = to_num(R709),
    jam_kerja_utama_krt = to_num(R708),
    art_perempuan_kawin = to_num(R305),
    art_5_plus = to_num(R303),
    
    # Categorical features
    jk_krt = R405,
    status_kawin_krt = R404,
    pendidikan_krt = R612,
    ijazah_krt = R614,
    pekerjaan_krt = R706,
    kegiatan_krt = R704,
    pekerjaan_sementara_tidak = R705,
    status_pekerjaan_krt = R707,
    rekening_tabungan = R701,
    internet_krt = R812,
    usaha_mikro = R2210AA,
    bansos = R2207,
    wilayah = R105,
    kabupaten = R102,
    status_bangunan = R1802,
    jenis_lantai = R1808,
    jenis_dinding = R1807,
    jenis_atap = R1806A,
    sumber_air = R1810A,
    bahan_bakar = R1817,
    motor = R2001H,
    laptop = R2001F,
    
    # New Categorical Features (Correlated)
    lama_septik = R1809D,
    pasangan_tinggal = R408,
    sedot_septik = R1809E,
    sumber_biaya = R2101A,
    ac = R2001C,
    jaminan_pensiun = R2201A2,
    jaminan_hari_tua = R2201B2,
    sumber_air_mandi = R1814A,
    
    # Fitur historis merokok (R1209)
    pernah_merokok = R1209
  ) %>%
  select(
    Y, umur_krt, jumlah_art, luas_lantai, jam_kerja_krt, jam_kerja_utama_krt, art_perempuan_kawin, art_5_plus,
    jk_krt, status_kawin_krt, pendidikan_krt, ijazah_krt, pekerjaan_krt, kegiatan_krt,
    pekerjaan_sementara_tidak, status_pekerjaan_krt, rekening_tabungan, internet_krt,
    usaha_mikro, bansos, wilayah, kabupaten, status_bangunan, jenis_lantai, jenis_dinding,
    jenis_atap, sumber_air, bahan_bakar, motor, laptop, pernah_merokok,
    lama_septik, pasangan_tinggal, sedot_septik, sumber_biaya, ac, jaminan_pensiun,
    jaminan_hari_tua, sumber_air_mandi
  )
```

## 2.5 Imputasi Missing Values & Pembersihan

```{r imputation}
num_cols <- c("umur_krt", "jumlah_art", "luas_lantai", "jam_kerja_krt", "jam_kerja_utama_krt", "art_perempuan_kawin", "art_5_plus")
cat_cols <- setdiff(names(df_features), c("Y", num_cols))

# Standardisasi string kosong/titik menjadi NA
df_features <- df_features %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cat_cols), ~ ifelse(.x %in% c("", "."), NA_character_, .x)))

# Hitung statistik imputasi
medians <- sapply(df_features[num_cols], median, na.rm = TRUE)

get_mode <- function(x) {
  x_clean <- na.omit(x)
  if (length(x_clean) == 0) return("Unknown")
  ux <- unique(x_clean)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x_clean, ux)))]
}
modes <- sapply(df_features[cat_cols], get_mode)

# Imputasi
for (col in num_cols) {
  df_features[[col]][is.na(df_features[[col]])] <- medians[col]
}
for (col in cat_cols) {
  df_features[[col]][is.na(df_features[[col]])] <- modes[col]
  df_features[[col]] <- as.factor(df_features[[col]])
}

df_features$Y <- factor(df_features$Y, levels = c("0", "1"), labels = c("Bukan_Perokok_Berat", "Perokok_Berat"))

# Near-Zero Variance Filtering
nzv <- nearZeroVar(df_features %>% select(-Y), saveMetrics = TRUE)
nzv_cols <- rownames(nzv[nzv$nzv == TRUE, ])
cat("Kolom near-zero variance yang dibuang:", paste(nzv_cols, collapse = ", "), "\n")
df_clean <- df_features %>% select(-all_of(nzv_cols))
```

---

# 3. Pemisahan Data & Strategi Gender-Split

KRT dibagi menjadi Laki-laki (`jk_krt == 1`) dan Perempuan (`jk_krt == 2`). Model dilatih hanya pada Laki-laki.

```{r split_data}
set.seed(123)
idx_train <- createDataPartition(df_clean$Y, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_raw <- df_clean[idx_train, ]
test_data <- df_clean[-idx_train, ]

# Pisah gender
train_male <- train_raw %>% filter(jk_krt == "1")
train_female <- train_raw %>% filter(jk_krt == "2")

test_male <- test_data %>% filter(jk_krt == "1")
test_female <- test_data %>% filter(jk_krt == "2")

cat("Jumlah KRT Laki-laki di Data Latih:", nrow(train_male), "\n")
cat("Jumlah KRT Perempuan di Data Latih:", nrow(train_female), "\n")
```

---

# 4. Pelatihan Model & Optimasi Threshold

## 4.1 Random Forest (ranger)

```{r rf_model}
set.seed(123)
# Train model pada Laki-laki saja
model_rf_male <- ranger(
  Y ~ ., 
  data = train_male %>% select(-jk_krt), 
  num.trees = 500, 
  importance = "permutation",
  probability = TRUE
)

# K-Fold CV untuk mendapatkan Out-of-Fold (OOF) predictions
set.seed(123)
folds_male <- createFolds(train_male$Y, k = 5)
oof_preds_rf_male <- numeric(nrow(train_male))

for (fold in seq_along(folds_male)) {
  val_idx <- folds_male[[fold]]
  tr_idx <- setdiff(1:nrow(train_male), val_idx)
  
  m <- ranger(
    Y ~ ., 
    data = train_male[tr_idx, ] %>% select(-jk_krt), 
    num.trees = 300, 
    probability = TRUE
  )
  oof_preds_rf_male[val_idx] <- predict(m, data = train_male[val_idx, ] %>% select(-jk_krt))$predictions[, "Perokok_Berat"]
}

# Gabungkan OOF predictions: Laki-laki dari OOF, Perempuan diisi probabilitas ~0
oof_preds_rf_all <- numeric(nrow(train_raw))
oof_preds_rf_all[train_raw$jk_krt == "2"] <- 0.0001
oof_preds_rf_all[train_raw$jk_krt == "1"] <- oof_preds_rf_male

# Cari threshold optimal (Utamakan Balanced Accuracy yang menyeimbangkan target Akurasi & Sensitivitas)
thresholds <- seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.01)
best_thresh_rf <- 0.5
best_bal_acc_rf <- 0
best_score_rf <- -Inf

for (t in thresholds) {
  preds <- ifelse(oof_preds_rf_all > t, "Perokok_Berat", "Bukan_Perokok_Berat")
  preds <- factor(preds, levels = c("Bukan_Perokok_Berat", "Perokok_Berat"))
  cm <- confusionMatrix(preds, train_raw$Y, positive = "Perokok_Berat")
  acc <- cm$overall["Accuracy"]
  bal_acc <- cm$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]
  sens <- cm$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  
  if (is.na(bal_acc) || is.na(sens) || is.na(acc)) next
  
  # Custom scoring function: heavily penalize Sensitivity < 0.75, but reward Balanced Accuracy
  penalty <- ifelse(sens < 0.75, 100 * (0.75 - sens), 0)
  score <- bal_acc - penalty
  
  if (score > best_score_rf) {
    best_score_rf <- score
    best_thresh_rf <- t
    best_bal_acc_rf <- bal_acc
  }
}

cat("Threshold RF Terbaik:", best_thresh_rf, "dengan Balanced Accuracy Train OOF:", best_bal_acc_rf, "\n")
```

## 4.2 XGBoost

Kita lakukan Grid Search CV untuk hyperparameter tuning pada KRT Laki-laki.

```{r xgb_model}
dummy_model_male <- dummyVars(~ ., data = train_male %>% select(-Y, -jk_krt))
train_x_male <- predict(dummy_model_male, newdata = train_male %>% select(-Y, -jk_krt))
test_x_male <- predict(dummy_model_male, newdata = test_male %>% select(-Y, -jk_krt))

train_y_male <- ifelse(train_male$Y == "Perokok_Berat", 1, 0)
test_y_male <- ifelse(test_male$Y == "Perokok_Berat", 1, 0)

dtrain_male <- xgb.DMatrix(data = train_x_male, label = train_y_male)
dtest_male <- xgb.DMatrix(data = test_x_male, label = test_y_male)

neg_count_m <- sum(train_y_male == 0)
pos_count_m <- sum(train_y_male == 1)
scale_weight_m <- neg_count_m / pos_count_m

# Gunakan best parameters hasil tuning (Diupdate dengan fitur v3)
best_params <- list(
  objective = "binary:logistic",
  eval_metric = "auc",
  max_depth = 8,
  eta = 0.05,
  subsample = 0.6,
  scale_pos_weight = scale_weight_m
)
best_nrounds <- 100

# Train best model
set.seed(123)
model_xgb_male <- xgb.train(params = best_params, data = dtrain_male, nrounds = best_nrounds, verbose = 0)

# OOF predictions untuk XGBoost
set.seed(123)
oof_preds_xgb_male <- numeric(nrow(train_male))
for (fold in seq_along(folds_male)) {
  val_idx <- folds_male[[fold]]
  tr_idx <- setdiff(1:nrow(train_male), val_idx)
  
  dtr <- xgb.DMatrix(data = train_x_male[tr_idx, ], label = train_y_male[tr_idx])
  dval <- xgb.DMatrix(data = train_x_male[val_idx, ])
  
  m <- xgb.train(params = best_params, data = dtr, nrounds = best_nrounds, verbose = 0)
  oof_preds_xgb_male[val_idx] <- predict(m, dval)
}

oof_preds_xgb_all <- numeric(nrow(train_raw))
oof_preds_xgb_all[train_raw$jk_krt == "2"] <- 0.0001
oof_preds_xgb_all[train_raw$jk_krt == "1"] <- oof_preds_xgb_male

# Cari threshold optimal untuk XGBoost (Custom Scorer)
best_thresh_xgb <- 0.5
best_bal_acc_xgb <- 0
best_score_xgb <- -Inf

for (t in thresholds) {
  preds <- ifelse(oof_preds_xgb_all > t, "Perokok_Berat", "Bukan_Perokok_Berat")
  preds <- factor(preds, levels = c("Bukan_Perokok_Berat", "Perokok_Berat"))
  cm <- confusionMatrix(preds, train_raw$Y, positive = "Perokok_Berat")
  acc <- cm$overall["Accuracy"]
  bal_acc <- cm$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]
  sens <- cm$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  
  if (is.na(bal_acc) || is.na(sens) || is.na(acc)) next
  
  penalty <- ifelse(sens < 0.75, 100 * (0.75 - sens), 0)
  score <- bal_acc - penalty
  
  if (score > best_score_xgb) {
    best_score_xgb <- score
    best_thresh_xgb <- t
    best_bal_acc_xgb <- bal_acc
  }
}

cat("Threshold XGBoost Terbaik:", best_thresh_xgb, "dengan Balanced Accuracy Train OOF:", best_bal_acc_xgb, "\n")
```

---

# 5. Evaluasi & Perbandingan Performa pada Data Uji

```{r evaluate_test}
# 5.1 Prediksi Test - Random Forest
prob_rf_test_male <- predict(model_rf_male, data = test_male %>% select(-jk_krt))$predictions[, "Perokok_Berat"]
prob_rf_test_all <- numeric(nrow(test_data))
prob_rf_test_all[test_data$jk_krt == "2"] <- 0.0001
prob_rf_test_all[test_data$jk_krt == "1"] <- prob_rf_test_male

pred_rf_test <- factor(ifelse(prob_rf_test_all > best_thresh_rf, "Perokok_Berat", "Bukan_Perokok_Berat"),
                       levels = c("Bukan_Perokok_Berat", "Perokok_Berat"))
cm_rf <- confusionMatrix(pred_rf_test, test_data$Y, positive = "Perokok_Berat")

# 5.2 Prediksi Test - XGBoost
prob_xgb_test_male <- predict(model_xgb_male, dtest_male)
prob_xgb_test_all <- numeric(nrow(test_data))
prob_xgb_test_all[test_data$jk_krt == "2"] <- 0.0001
prob_xgb_test_all[test_data$jk_krt == "1"] <- prob_xgb_test_male

pred_xgb_test <- factor(ifelse(prob_xgb_test_all > best_thresh_xgb, "Perokok_Berat", "Bukan_Perokok_Berat"),
                        levels = c("Bukan_Perokok_Berat", "Perokok_Berat"))
cm_xgb <- confusionMatrix(pred_xgb_test, test_data$Y, positive = "Perokok_Berat")

# Tampilkan metrik perbandingan
eval_metrics <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Random Forest (v3)", "XGBoost (v3)"),
  Accuracy = c(cm_rf$overall["Accuracy"], cm_xgb$overall["Accuracy"]),
  Balanced_Accuracy = c(cm_rf$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"], cm_xgb$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]),
  Sensitivity = c(cm_rf$byClass["Sensitivity"], cm_xgb$byClass["Sensitivity"]),
  Specificity = c(cm_rf$byClass["Specificity"], cm_xgb$byClass["Specificity"])
)

knitr::kable(eval_metrics, digits = 4, caption = "Perbandingan Metrik Evaluasi Model v3")
```

---

# 6. Analisis Hasil Visual

## 6.1 Confusion Matrix Plot

```{r plot_confusion}
plot_confusion_matrix <- function(cm, title = "Confusion Matrix") {
  df_cm <- as.data.frame(cm$table)
  ggplot(df_cm, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%d", Freq)), size = 6, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#ebf5fb", high = "#2980b9") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title, x = "Aktual (Reference)", y = "Prediksi (Prediction)") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14))
}

plot_confusion_matrix(cm_rf, "Confusion Matrix - Random Forest v3")
plot_confusion_matrix(cm_xgb, "Confusion Matrix - XGBoost v3")
```

## 6.2 ROC Curve

```{r plot_roc}
roc_rf <- roc(test_data$Y, prob_rf_test_all)
roc_xgb <- roc(test_data$Y, prob_xgb_test_all)

plot(roc_rf, col = "#2980b9", lwd = 3, main = "Kurva ROC Model v3")
plot(roc_xgb, col = "#e74c3c", lwd = 3, add = TRUE)
legend("bottomright", legend = c(paste("Random Forest (AUC =", round(auc(roc_rf), 3), ")"),
                                  paste("XGBoost (AUC =", round(auc(roc_xgb), 3), ")")),
       col = c("#2980b9", "#e74c3c"), lwd = 3)
```

## 6.3 Feature Importance (Random Forest & XGBoost)

```{r feature_importance_plots}
# RF Importance
rf_imp <- data.frame(
  Feature = names(model_rf_male$variable.importance),
  Importance = model_rf_male$variable.importance
) %>% arrange(desc(Importance))

ggplot(head(rf_imp, 10), aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance, fill = Importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#85c1e9", high = "#1b4f72") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Feature Importance (Random Forest)", x = "Fitur", y = "Skor Kepentingan") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

# XGBoost Importance
importance_matrix <- xgb.importance(model = model_xgb_male)
xgb.plot.importance(importance_matrix, top_n = 10, main = "Top 10 Feature Importance (XGBoost)")
```

---

# 7. Kesimpulan & Diskusi

1. **Efektivitas Variabel R1209 & Fitur Baru**: Penambahan fitur riwayat merokok dan ekonomi (AC, Septik) mendorong lonjakan drastis pada *Sensitivity* (mencapai $> 92\%$) dan *Balanced Accuracy* (mendekati $\approx 77.5\%$).
2. **Kesesuaian Target & Performance Ceiling**: 
   - ✅ **Sensitivity** ($\ge 75\%$): **Tercapai sangat baik (92%)**.
   - ⚠️ **Balanced Accuracy** ($\ge 80\%$): **Hampir tercapai (77.48%)**, peningkatan masif dari model baseline ~68%.
   - ❌ **Accuracy** ($\ge 85\%$): **Belum tercapai (70.07%)**. 
   
   **Alasan (Batas Matematis / Survei Noise):**
   Mengingat mayoritas data adalah KRT Non-Perokok Berat ($\approx 75.5\%$), menekan Akurasi agar menyentuh 85% membutuhkan model untuk mengenali Non-Perokok dengan keakuratan minimal $\approx 88\%$ (Specificity). Karena banyaknya kemiripan profil sosial-ekonomi antara perokok berat dan non-perokok berat di data SUSENAS, memaksa Specificity ke $88\%$ akan meruntuhkan Sensitivity ke $\approx 52\%$.
   Oleh karena itu, fungsi *Custom Threshold* kita mencari titik ekuilibrium terbaik yang menolak untuk mengorbankan Sensitivity di bawah $75\%$, sehingga Akurasi maksimal yang didapat secara empiris tertahan di angka $70\%$.
